name: summarize-task-context-never-actually-fired
tags: summarize_task_context, task context summary, BareClaudeAgent, threshold, logs, verified, first-turn-gated, ups-context-sources-all-capped
body:
summarize_task_context runs every UPS turn but its first-turn-gated inner logic (raw context length check) has only fired 3 times total in claude_hooks.sqlite history (191/306/143 chars) — all under the 800-char threshold, so BareClaudeAgent has never actually been invoked. No "invoking BareClaudeAgent" or "summary=... chars" log line exists anywhere.

Why: [[ups-context-sources-all-capped]] says this node was "re-enabled... is now live again" — true in the sense that it runs, but it has never once produced a non-empty task_context_summary in practice. Confirmed by reading claude_hooks.sqlite via hooks__read_logs_sqlite, not assumed from code alone.

How to apply: don't assume "gated and wired correctly" means "actually exercised" — before relying on a node's real-world behavior (not just its code path), grep the actual log DB for its success-path log lines, same as done here. If task activations ever accumulate >800 chars of related-task/RAG/commit context on a first turn, this will fire for the first time — worth checking again if that ever seems to matter.
